tasks.title: Create tagger agent definition file
tasks.description: Write the agent definition at ~/.claude/agents/tagger.md with allowedTools restricted to mcp__prism__kanban_list_tasks, mcp__prism__kanban_update_task, mcp__prism__kanban_get_task. Include the system prompt that instructs the agent on classification rules and the 4 valid types.
acceptance_criteria: File exists at ~/.claude/agents/tagger.md
YAML frontmatter has allowedTools listing only the 3 mcp__prism__* tools
Body contains clear classification instructions for feature, bug, tech-debt, chore
No shell, file, or web tools appear in allowedTools

tasks.title: Add @anthropic-ai/sdk dependency to backend
tasks.description: Add '@anthropic-ai/sdk' ^0.30.0 to the root package.json dependencies and run npm install. Verify the installed SDK version is compatible with the messages API used in T-003.
acceptance_criteria: '@anthropic-ai/sdk' appears in package.json dependencies
npm install succeeds with no peer-dependency warnings
node -e "require('@anthropic-ai/sdk')" exits with code 0
package-lock.json is updated and committed

tasks.title: Create src/prompts/tagger-system.txt
tasks.description: Write the static system prompt for the tagger (see blueprint §5.2). The prompt must define the 4 types with their definitions, output schema rules, confidence levels, and description rewriting instructions. It must instruct Claude to respond ONLY with JSON matching the defined schema.
acceptance_criteria: File exists at src/prompts/tagger-system.txt
Prompt defines all 4 types: feature, bug, tech-debt, chore
Prompt specifies the exact JSON response schema
Prompt instructs Claude to use temperature=0 behavior (deterministic)
Prompt handles improveDescriptions=true and false cases
Prompt includes a 'skipped' array for unclassifiable cards

tasks.title: Implement src/handlers/tagger.js
tasks.description: Create the tagger route handler. Validates the request body (column, improveDescriptions). Reads tasks from disk for the given spaceId (using the existing readColumn pattern from tasks.js). Calls the Anthropic SDK messages.create() with the system prompt from T-003 and a user message containing the card JSON. Parses and validates the response. Returns { suggestions, skipped, model, inputTokens, outputTokens }. Guards against concurrent runs per spaceId using a module-level Set. Returns 503 if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is missing, 409 if already running, 502 on Anthropic error.
acceptance_criteria: Handler file exists at src/handlers/tagger.js
Returns 503 ANTHROPIC_KEY_MISSING when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var is absent
Returns 404 SPACE_NOT_FOUND when spaceId does not exist
Returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR when column is not one of todo|in-progress|done
Returns 409 TAGGER_ALREADY_RUNNING when a run is already in progress for that spaceId
Returns 200 with { suggestions, skipped, model, inputTokens, outputTokens } on success
Emits structured log line on completion and on error (see blueprint §7)
Does not expose ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in any response field or log
Claude SDK call uses temperature: 0
Response is validated before returning — invalid JSON from Claude returns 502

tasks.title: Register tagger route in server.js
tasks.description: Add the route pattern /^//api/v1/spaces/([^/]+)/tagger/run$/ to server.js main router. Wire POST to handleTaggerRun from src/handlers/tagger.js. Route must be placed BEFORE the catch-all SPACES_TASKS_ROUTE to avoid regex swallowing.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/spaces/:spaceId/tagger/run is reachable (returns non-404)
Route is declared before SPACES_TASKS_ROUTE in the router
Other existing routes are unaffected (regression test passes)
GET on the same path returns 405 METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED

tasks.title: Add runTagger() to frontend API client
tasks.description: Add the typed runTagger(spaceId, opts) function to frontend/src/api/client.ts. Define TaggerSuggestion, TaggerResult, TaggerOptions, and TaggerState TypeScript interfaces in frontend/src/types/index.ts. The function calls POST /api/v1/spaces/:spaceId/tagger/run and returns TaggerResult.
acceptance_criteria: runTagger(spaceId, opts) is exported from api/client.ts
TaggerSuggestion, TaggerResult, TaggerOptions types exist in types/index.ts
TypeScript compiles with no errors (npm run build in frontend/)
Function passes improveDescriptions and column in the request body
Function re-throws errors as Error objects with the server's message

tasks.title: Add tagger state slice to useAppStore
tasks.description: Extend frontend/src/stores/useAppStore.ts with a taggerState slice. Fields: taggerLoading (boolean), taggerSuggestions (TaggerSuggestion[]), taggerModalOpen (boolean), taggerError (string | null). Actions: startTagger(), setSuggestions(result), closeTagger(), setTaggerError(message). startTagger disables the button; closeTagger clears suggestions and closes the modal.
acceptance_criteria: taggerLoading initializes to false
startTagger() sets taggerLoading=true and taggerSuggestions=[]
setSuggestions() sets taggerSuggestions and opens taggerModalOpen=true
closeTagger() resets taggerLoading, taggerSuggestions=[], taggerModalOpen=false
setTaggerError() stores the message and sets taggerLoading=false
TypeScript compiles with no errors

tasks.title: Implement TaggerButton component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/board/TaggerButton.tsx. Renders a ghost Button (reuse shared Button component) with a sparkles/auto-fix icon. Shows a spinner when taggerLoading=true. On click, calls runTagger() with the active spaceId. On success, dispatches setSuggestions(). On error, dispatches setTaggerError(). Button is disabled while taggerLoading=true. Integrate the button into the Board header (frontend/src/components/board/Board.tsx), next to the existing action buttons.
acceptance_criteria: TaggerButton.tsx exists in frontend/src/components/board/
Button uses the shared <Button variant='ghost'> component
Button is disabled and shows a spinner during taggerLoading=true
Clicking the button while NOT loading calls runTagger() exactly once
On success, useAppStore.setSuggestions() is called with the result
On error, useAppStore.setTaggerError() is called with the error message
Button is visible in the Board header in both light and dark theme
TypeScript compiles with no errors

tasks.title: Implement TaggerReviewModal component
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/components/modals/TaggerReviewModal.tsx. Uses the shared Modal component. Displays each suggestion in a card row: current badge, arrow, inferred badge, title, confidence indicator. If improveDescriptions was true, shows a diff of old vs new description inline. Each row has an accept/reject toggle (default: accept). Footer: 'Apply selected (N)' primary button and 'Cancel' ghost button. On Apply: iterate accepted suggestions, call api.updateTask() for each, then call refreshBoard() and closeTagger(). Shows a progress state (applying X of N) during bulk apply. Errors per card are shown inline without blocking the rest.
acceptance_criteria: TaggerReviewModal.tsx exists in frontend/src/components/modals/
Uses shared <Modal> component (handles Escape, backdrop, focus trap)
Each suggestion row shows: current type badge, arrow icon, inferred type badge, card title
Each row has an accept/reject toggle; all start as accepted by default
Low-confidence suggestions are visually distinguished (e.g., amber indicator)
Apply button label shows count of selected suggestions
Apply button calls PUT /tasks/:id for each accepted suggestion sequentially
Cancel closes the modal without making any API calls
After Apply completes, board is refreshed (refreshBoard() called)
Modal follows dark-theme-default design system (Tailwind tokens only, no inline styles)

tasks.title: Write backend unit tests for tagger handler
tasks.description: Create tests/tagger.test.js using the existing Node.js test runner pattern (node:test). Mock the Anthropic SDK to avoid real API calls. Cover: missing API key → 503, invalid column → 400, space not found → 404, concurrent runs → 409, successful run → 200 with correct suggestion shape, Claude returns invalid JSON → 502, empty board (no tasks) → 200 with empty suggestions.
acceptance_criteria: tests/tagger.test.js exists and runs with 'npm test'
All 7 scenarios listed in the description have test cases
Tests do NOT make real HTTP calls to Anthropic API (SDK is mocked)
npm test exits with code 0
Line coverage for src/handlers/tagger.js >= 90%

tasks.title: Write frontend unit tests for TaggerReviewModal
tasks.description: Create frontend/__tests__/components/TaggerReviewModal.test.tsx using Vitest + React Testing Library. Cover: renders all suggestions, accept/reject toggle works, Apply button count updates when rows are toggled, Apply calls updateTask for each accepted row, Cancel closes modal without API calls, empty suggestions state (no cards changed by Claude) shows appropriate message.
acceptance_criteria: TaggerReviewModal.test.tsx exists in frontend/__tests__/components/
All 6 scenarios listed in the description have test cases
Tests run with 'cd frontend && npm test' and pass
API calls in Apply flow are mocked (no real fetch)
Component coverage >= 80%

tasks.title: Validate ANTHROPIC_API_KEY on server startup and document setup
tasks.description: Add a startup check in server.js: if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is absent, log a warning '[tagger] ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set — tagger endpoint will return 503'. Do NOT block server start. Add a one-liner to the CLAUDE.md project docs under 'Starting the app' explaining the env var is needed for the tagger feature. This is a documentation + safety-net task.
acceptance_criteria: Server starts normally without ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (no crash)
A warning log line is emitted at startup when key is absent
CLAUDE.md mentions ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in the Starting the app section
Log line does NOT print the key value even partially
